title: Contributing to dsVert
text: Thank considering contribution. dsVert server-side half tightly-paired R + Go codebase implements federated MPC primitives vertically partitioned DataSHIELD analyses. Contributions need clear three quality bars: correctness (matches centralised R within documented bounds), non-disclosure (observation-level data ever leaves server), reviewer-grade hygiene (R CMD check clean, testthat green, go test green).

title: Contributing to dsVert
text: R/ — exported dsvert*DS / k2*DS / glmRing63*DS / psi*DS aggregate methods assign methods. Hand-maintained NAMESPACE (regenerate); add new exports manually. inst/dsvert-mpc/ — Go MPC kernel sources. Compiled per platform inst/bin/{darwin-amd64,darwin-arm64,linux-amd64,windows-amd64}/. inst/mhe-tool/ — legacy MHE tool (CKKS); kept backward-compat. tests/testthat/ — server-side unit tests (K-arity guards, contingency, histogram, LMM K=2 enforcement, etc.).

title: Contributing to dsVert
text: Fork create feature branch main. Match existing style: don’t reformat adjacent code. New R/ files use roxygen2 markdown mode (set DESCRIPTION); wrap math \eqn{} / \preformatted{}, never bare LaTeX macros. Document every exported argument — R CMD check ---cran gates . Use \code{} (bracket-link prose) inside markdown roxygen, use call(name = "fn", ...) (explicit name = ... form) client-side DSI::datashield.aggregate call expressions avoid partial-arg-match NOTE. Run local validation gate pushing: method ships new MPC orchestration, add probe scripts/probe_<method>.R wire continuous_validation.sh. R CMD check: Acceptance: Status: 0 ERRORs / 1 WARNING / 0 NOTEs — single warning intentional inst/bin/{platform}/dsvert-mpc Go runtime binaries, unavoidable dual-package R + Go architecture. new warnings notes must addressed. testthat: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' — full suite must PASS. Add tests new method. Go tests: cd inst/dsvert-mpc && go test ./... — must PASS ~2 s. Submit pull request referencing related issue. CI workflow (.github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml) reproduce check clean Linux runner.
code: bash scripts/quick_impl_check.sh        # ~3 min  (8 L1 probes + go test) bash scripts/continuous_validation.sh medium   # ~33 min  (L1 + L2 local-distributed) R CMD build --no-build-vignettes dsVert R CMD check dsVert_*.tar.gz --no-manual --as-cran

title: dsVert
text: dsVert server-side DataSHIELD package privacy-preserving statistical analysis vertically partitioned federated data. server holds different features (columns) observations (rows). server sees another’s raw data. computation uses Ring63 / Ring127 Beaver MPC — pure fixed-point arithmetic additive secret sharing — combined DCF wide-spline non-linear link functions, OT-Beaver triple generation, ECDH-PSI record alignment. observation-level data ever disclosed. Pair client-side companion package dsVertClient.

title: dsVert
text: client sees: p-dimensional gradient aggregates, correlation scalars, deviance (1 scalar), per-cluster BLUPs, χ² statistics. individual observations. server sees: data + random additive Ring shares servers’ data. reconstruct without shares.
code: Client (analyst)                    Servers (data holders) ┌──────────────┐                   ┌──────────────────────┐ │ ds.vertGLM() │──── DataSHIELD ──→│ Ring63/Ring127 shares│ │ ds.vertCox() │    (HTTP/REST)    │ Beaver matvec        │ │ ds.vertLMM() │                   │ DCF wide-spline      │ │ ds.psiAlign()│←── p aggregates ──│ OT-Beaver triples    │ │ ds.vertNB()  │   (gradients,     │ Ed25519 verification │ │ ds.vertCor() │    deviance)      │ X25519 + AES-256-GCM │ └──────────────┘                   └──────────────────────┘

title: dsVert
text: Standalone binary providing Ring63 / Ring127 MPC kernels: DCF wide-spline (sigmoid / exp / reciprocal / log-sum-exp), Beaver triple generation, OT-Beaver dishonest-majority triples, transport encryption (X25519 + AES-256-GCM), Ed25519 identity verification, Catrina-Saxena 2010 fixed-point fp50 ULP truncation. Pure Go, external crypto libraries beyond golang.org/x/crypto. Per-platform binaries ship inst/bin/{darwin-amd64,darwin-arm64,linux-amd64,windows-amd64}/.

what: Cox one-step Newton at beta = 0 (bias-free) — cox-newton
title: Cox one-step Newton at beta = 0 (bias-free) — cox-newton
text: beta = 0, mu = exp(X*beta) = 1 identically. Neither DCF exp spline DCF reciprocal spline invoked, score expected Fisher information EXACT Ring63 FP ring (apart ~1e-5 FP quantisation per op). single Newton step beta_hat = inv(Fisher(0)) . grad(0) recovers Cox MLE O(|beta|^2) – within 5-10% small coefficients, plenty paper's inference bar. Closed-form expressions beta = 0: Ring63 shares LINEAR: reverse cumsum party's share share reverse cumsum reconstructed column. Plain- text--weights (delta/N, delta/N^2) can folded via standard share * plaintext elementwise product. non-linear step X_j * X_k S_j * S_k, handled existing Beaver vecmul primitive (k2BeaverVecmul Gen / Consume / R1 / R2 DS). Pre-conditions set ds.vertCox setup calling path: * k2ShareInputDS(..) populated k2_x_share_fp () k2_peer_x_share_fp (peer cols) row-major FP. * k2SetCoxTimesDS / k2ReceiveCoxMetaDS broadcast sort permutation + delta PLAINTEXT FP (bytes parties) ss$k2_cox_delta_fp, permutation applied via k2ApplyCoxPermutationDS shares sorted t. * strata (optional) cached ss$k2_cox_strata. Inter-server disclosure: unchanged iterative Cox pipeline – permutation delta already disclosed setup. new channels, per-observation reveal. reveals scalar aggregates (p-vector grad, p*p Fisher).
code: grad_j(0)      = sum_{i: delta_i=1} ( X_ij - S_j(t_i) / N(t_i) )     Fisher_jk(0)   = sum_{i: delta_i=1} ( S_jk(t_i)/N(t_i)                                           - S_j(t_i)*S_k(t_i)/N(t_i)^2 )   with S_j(t_i)  = sum_{m >= i} X_mj  (reverse cumsum after sort by t)        S_jk(t_i) = sum_{m >= i} X_mj X_mk                (ditto)        N(t_i)    = abs(R(t_i)) = n - i + 1 (unique-ties; strata reset).

what: Cox Path B: iterative Newton with Fisher(beta_k) via Beaver — cox-path-b
title: Cox Path B: iterative Newton with Fisher(beta_k) via Beaver — cox-path-b
text: Server-side helpers iterative Newton refinement path computes observed Fisher information (beta_k) current iterate using primitives already exist Ring63 + Beaver + DCF toolkit. new Go primitives. Strategy iteration k: 1. .cox_score_round(beta_k) populated session state shares mu = exp(X beta_k), G, S, 1/S, muG (via existing DCF + Beaver infrastructure). 2. column j: Beaver(X_j_share * mu_share) -> share X_jmu per row. local reverse cumsum (strata-aware) gives share T_j() = sum k >= X_kj mu_k. 3. One Beaver((1/S) * (1/S)) gives share 1/S^2 per row. 4. Per Fisher pair (j, k): Term1 = sum_m X_mj X_mk (mu G)_m = Beaver(X_j * X_k)  ->  Beaver(result * mu*G)  ->  sum Term2 = sum_i delta_i T_j() T_k() / S(t_i)^2 = Beaver(T_j * T_k)  ->  Beaver(result * 1/S^2)  -> multiply delta (plaintext )  ->  sum Fisher_jk = Term1 - Term2 (scalar aggregate revealed client). Bias cancellation: grad(beta_k) Fisher(beta_k) computed DCF-spline + Beaver pipeline, ~1-3% multiplicative bias per spline evaluation appears numerator denominator Newton step, cancelling leading order (Greenland 1987, Therneau & Grambsch 2000 Sect 3.2). Disclosure per iter (tier Newton one-step): client sees p-vector grad(beta_k) p*p Fisher(beta_k). Max 5 iters capped client.

title: Cox K=2 discrete-time non-disclosive share-mask primitives (#D') — dsvertCoxDiscreteShareMaskDS
text: Worker2 K=2-safe option (B) – hide J_i (per-patient ending bin index) covariate (non-label) server. Implements share-mask gating pattern documented project_k2_strict_unified_plan_2026-04-27.md Sec."Option B FEASIBILITY ANALYSIS": outcome (label) server, J_i status_i local plaintext. Compute per-patient -risk mask m_ij {0,1} (j=1..J, =1..n) m_ij = (j <= J_i) event indicator y_ij = (j == J_i status_i = 1). m y get split Ring127 additive shares OS covariate server. covariate server thereby never sees J_i directly; signal receives two random-looking length-(J*n) shares , summed pointwise mod 2^127 OS shares, reconstruct m y. covariate server expands X UNIFORM Jxn person-period frame (every patient contributes J rows, regardless true J_i), row-count signal leaks. mask shares gate rows enter score / Hessian aggregations downstream via Beaver vecmul (y - p) W = p*(1-p). Cite: Aliasgari-Blanton 2013 NDSS eprint 2012/405 (share-mask gating); Cock et al. 2016 eprint 2016/736 (oblivious selection); Mohassel-Zhang 2017 IEEE S&P eprint 2017/396 SecureML; Andreux et al. 2020 arXiv:2006.08997 (discrete-time Cox MLE pooled-logistic); Allison 1982 Sociological Methodology 13:61-98 (canonical pooled- logistic equivalence discrete Cox); Catrina-Saxena 2010 FC2010 (Ring127 frac=50 truncation noise floor).

what: Non-disclosive K=2 share-domain primitives for NB full-reg theta MLE — nb-full-reg-share
title: Non-disclosive K=2 share-domain primitives for NB full-reg theta MLE — nb-full-reg-share
text: Server-side functions implementing non-disclosive variant ds.vertNBFullRegTheta(variant="full_reg_nd") per spec docs/error_bounds/nb_t2_log_primitive_close_well_2026-04-29.md. Closes D-INV-4 violation prior full_reg variant (transport-decrypted eta^nl plaintext label server) keeping eta^nl Ring127 additive secret shares end--end share-domain mu + log(mu+theta) + 1/(theta+mu) Beaver pipeline. Threat model: K=2 OT-Beaver dishonest-majority (Demmler-Schneider- Zohner ABY 2015 Sec.III.B). Inter-server channels Ed25519-pinned via trusted_peers (mpcUtils.R:530-556). entry points K=2-via .k2_enforce_K(ss, 2L, ...). Refs: Lawless 1987 Can. J. Statist. 15(3):209-225 (NB profile-MLE theta score); Venables & Ripley 2002 MASS Sec.7.4 (glm.nb Newton); Catrina & Saxena 2010 Financial Cryptography Sec.3.3 (multiplicative depth ULP propagation); Trefethen ATAP Sec.8 (Bernstein-ellipse rel error bound); Beaver 1991 CRYPTO Sec.3 (precomputed multiplication triples); Boyle, Couteau & Gilboa 2019 (DCFNet – future per-element argument reduction).

what: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment - Server-Side Functions (Blind Relay) — psi-protocol
title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment - Server-Side Functions (Blind Relay) — psi-protocol
text: functions implement Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman Private Set Intersection (ECDH-PSI) privacy-preserving record alignment across vertically partitioned data. EC point exchanges transport-encrypted (X25519 + AES-256-GCM ECIES) client acts blind relay, never seeing raw elliptic curve points.

title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment - Server-Side Functions (Blind Relay) — psi-protocol
text: protocol exploits commutativity scalar multiplication P-256: \(\alpha \cdot (\beta \cdot H(id)) = \beta \cdot (\alpha \cdot H(id))\). Security (DDH assumption P-256, malicious-client model): client sees opaque encrypted blobs (reversible) server's scalar never leaves server PSI firewall enforces phase ordering one-shot semantics party can perform dictionary attacks OPRF oracle attacks

title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment - Server-Side Functions (Blind Relay) — psi-protocol

Cox Newton path — dsvertCoxNewton{Prep,Grad,LoadPair,FisherScalar}DS closed-form Fisher(0)/grad(0) beta=0 plus refinement steps; dsvertCoxPathBCumsumDS / dsvertCoxPathBScalarDS / dsvertCoxPathBCopyDS damped fixed-Fisher refinement P3 disclosure budget (HARD CAP 5 iters). NB regression — full profile-MLE digamma chain (dsvertNBProfileSumsDS, dsvertNBMomentSumsDS, dsvertNBEtaSealDS/dsvertNBEtaShareDS, dsvertNBFullScoreDS, dsvertNBSumShareDS, dsvertNBPsiAggregateDS) supporting iid-mu full-regression theta estimators Ring127 NR-LOG share-domain primitive (Goldschmidt 1964 + Pugh 2004). Multinomial joint Newton — dsvertPrepareMultinomGradDS, dsvertSoftmaxDenominatorDS, dsvertOneHotDS, dsvertComputeResidualShareDS K-1 stacked Bohning-bounded Newton path (paper §V.row). Ordinal joint PO Newton — dsvertOrdinalPatientDiffsDS, dsvertOrdinalSealFkSharesDS, dsvertOrdinalSealEtaDS, dsvertOrdinalReceiveBetaWeightsDS, dsvertOrdinalExtractXColumnDS Tutz 1990 §3.2 block-diagonal joint Newton + McCullagh §2.5 closed-form H_θθ. LMM (random intercept + slopes) — dsvertCluster{Sizes,Residuals}DS, dsvertExpandClusterWeightsDS, dsvertClusterZtZDS, dsvertLMMPeerFittedShareDS, dsvertLMMCoordResidualShareDS, dsvertLMMPeerResidualFinaliseDS, dsvertLMMExactClusterR2DS, dsvertLMM{Broadcast,Receive}ClusterIDsDS, dsvertLMM{Per,Global}SumDS, dsvertLMMGLS{Transform,Aggregates}DS, dsvertLMMLocalGramDS, dsvertLMMReceiveGramSharesDS, dsvertLMM{R1,R2}DS Laird-Ware GLS closed form Pinheiro-Bates §2.4.2 within-ANOVA variance components. LMM K=3 — dsvertLMMVarianceComponentsDS + dsvertLMMXCovarianceWithinDS K=3 sigma^2 / sigma_b^2 recovery Var_within(X β) correction. Beaver vecmul Ring63 / Ring127 stack — k2BeaverVecmul{GenTriples,ConsumeTriple,R1,R2}DS, k2Beaver{ShareVector,ReceiveVector,ExtractColumn,SumShare}DS, k2Ring127{AffineCombine,LocalScale}DS. Underpins joint Newton, Cox Path B, LMM Gram, chi-square primitives.
